rulings.Case Title: YASSER KHAIRI VS. RICHARD THOMAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANTS EDWARD WONG and MARGARET WONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied, without prejudice. (CCP § 1014.) Defendants Edward Wong and Margaret Wong (collectively "Defendants") failed to serve their motion for summary judgment on all parties who have appeared in this action. On December 5, 2012, Defendant Richard Thomas appeared in this action by filing his answer. (CCP § 1014. See Answer, filed Dec. 5, 2012.) "After appearance, a defendant or the defendant's attorney is entitled to notice of all subsequent proceedings of which notice is required to be given." (Id.) Defendants served their motion for summary judgment on Plaintiff, but failed to serve Defendant Richard Thomas. (See Notice of Motion Proof of Service, executed on Dec. 18, 2013, p. 6:17, 7:3-7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LORI WILLIAMS VS. WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, line 1, PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' motion To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defendants Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied for failure to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CINDY QIWANG XU VS. THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, line 2, DEFENDANT RAUL GUILLERMO Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena; Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The Court finds that Plaintiff has not shown a compelling interest to override Mr. Guillermo's right of privacy to his employment records at a former employer for which he worked five years before the incident in question. Speculation that the Defendant engaged in improper conduct at the prior employer does not establish a compelling need or relevance. Harding Lawson v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal. App. 4th 7. rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Defts' Deposition Subpoena To Marshall Medical Center
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, line 4, PLAINTIFF MOTAK, JEREMIAH JOSEPH A/K/A JERRY MOTAK Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Defts' Deposition Subpoena To Marshall Medical Center Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. The Court agrees that Defendant is entitled to the medical records at issue regarding any "internal bleeding, ulcers or stomach complaints, tests, treatment or medical opinions" from 9/25/01 to present. However, the Court finds that the request for records relating to "any physical disability" to be vastly overbroad. rulings.Case Title: HELGA KATRINA HORNER VS. DARIN GEISE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Alter Ego Claims From Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, Line 10, CROSS COMPLAINANTS DARIN GEISE and COUP D'ETAT, INC.'S Motion To Strike Alter Ego Claims From Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint. The motion to strike is denied. Paragraphs 8-10 plead sufficient facts to support an alter ego theory of liability. While the 2nd Amended Complaint does not expressly allege that the corporation is undercapitalized, plaintiff implicitly makes that allegation by alleging that the corporation is a mere shell (par. 8 of the 2nd Amended Complaint). The Court liberally construes plaintiff's allegations pursuant to CCP 452. rulings.Case Title: ROCKY POINT PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. ERIN THOMPSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT ERIN THOMPSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite any facts or law demonstrating that the parties shared a fiduciary relationship. An employer/employee or principal/agent relationship, standing alone, does not create a fiduciary relationship. See Odorizzi v. Bloomfield School Dist. (1966) 246 Cal.App.2d 123, 129. Plaintiff does not allege facts showing that it reposed trust and confidence in defendant. If plaintiff relies on paragraph 5 of the parties' employment contract (trade secrets), then plaintiff can only allege a claim under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act because that Act preempts all common law claims. Plaintiff's reference to paragraph 9 of the parties' agreement (noncompetition) does not show the existence of a fiduciary relationship. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2005-3, A VS. HEATHER BARTELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT HEATHER BARTELL'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion is granted. Plaintiff has the burden of proof of establishing jurisdiction, including proper substitute service. See Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup. Crt. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1222, 1232. Plaintiff fails to show that defendant was substitute served at her usual abode within the meaning of CCP 415.20. Defendant's declaration indicates that the address of service was no longer accurate at the time of service. rulings.Case Title: ROCKY POINT PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. YAT-PANG AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANTS RUSSIAN HILL ASSOCIATES, LLC, YAT-PANG AU, RUSSIAN HILL GROUP, LLC, NOB HILL VISTAS, LLC, EUREKA PARK, LLC, YAT-HON AU, YAT-GAI AU and PARNASSUS PLACE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. The Court does not rule on Defendants' request for judicial notice since it has been withdrawn. (Reply Brief at pg. 4, line 27- pg. 5, line 14.) As to the first cause of action, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") fails to plead facts establishing that it is not barred by the statute of frauds. (Civ. Code sec. 1624(a)(4).) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts, if it can in good faith, that the "services rendered" were those other than listing properties for sale. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on the same ground, since it seeks the same recovery as the first cause of action. "When a common count is used as an alternative way of seeking the same recovery demanded in a specific cause of action, and is based on the same facts, the common count is demurrable if the cause of action is demurrable." (McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 394.) As to the third cause of action, the FAC does not plead facts with the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
